title: Urban Gardens
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Urban gardens are (0) becoming a common sight in cities worldwide as residents seek to reconnect with nature and grow their own food. These green spaces range from small balcony gardens to large community plots shared by neighbourhood residents.||The benefits of urban gardens extend (9) beyond just providing fresh produce. They create opportunities for people to meet and work together, strengthening community bonds. Studies have shown that gardening can (10) also improve mental health by reducing stress and providing a sense of accomplishment. For children growing up in cities, urban gardens offer valuable lessons (11) about where food comes from and the importance of caring for the environment.||Many cities now recognise the value of these spaces and (12) are taking steps to support them through grants and land allocation. Some urban gardens focus (13) on growing vegetables and herbs, while others cultivate flowers to attract pollinators like bees and butterflies. Despite limited space, creative solutions (14) such as vertical gardens and container planting allow city dwellers to maximise their growing potential. (15) Whether you have a small windowsill or a large plot, urban gardening offers (16) an accessible way to contribute to a greener, more sustainable urban environment.

example.text: Urban gardens are (0) a common sight in cities worldwide.

questions.explanation: Preposition 'beyond' means further than certain benefits.
Adverb 'also' adds another benefit of urban gardening.
Preposition 'about' introduces the subject of the lessons.
Present continuous: 'are taking steps' indicates a current action.
Expression 'focus on' means to concentrate on something specific.
Expression 'such as' introduces examples of creative solutions.
Indefinite article before a word with a vowel sound: 'an accessible way'.
